user_query:
I need a daily scheduled job that helps me track and act on new critic reviews for our upcoming shows. Please implement and wire up a small pipeline that, once per day, queries the public web for reviews, ranks the findings, and generates meeting notes with actionable next steps for my team.

Inputs (already provided):
- input/shows.csv — columns: show_id, show_name, opening_date
- input/publications.json — fields:
  - priority_publications: an array of objects {name, domain} indicating outlets we care about most
  - keywords: an array of strings to bias queries (e.g., ["review", "critique", "premiere", "opening night"]).

Requirements:
1) Internet search:
   - For each show in input/shows.csv, construct multiple search queries combining the show_name with keywords and publication names (from input/publications.json). Use a major public search engine; no API keys. Do not hardcode a specific engine or a single URL. For each query, collect a reasonable number of organic results (e.g., up to 10), capturing at minimum: url, page/domain, title, snippet/summary text if available.
   - Save all raw results to outputs/raw/search_results.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with fields: query_id, show_id, show_name, query, url, domain, title, snippet, fetched_at (ISO 8601), and a boolean publication_match that is true if the result domain matches any priority_publications.domain.

2) Filter, sort, and rank:
   - From outputs/raw/search_results.jsonl, deduplicate obvious duplicates per show (same url or same title+domain).
   - Compute a simple score per result using only locally determinable features:
     - +2 if publication_match is true (domain matches one of the priority publications provided).
     - +1 if the title or snippet contains any of the input keywords (case-insensitive).
     - +1 if the show_name appears in the title (case-insensitive).
   - Produce outputs/aggregated/top_reviews.csv with columns: show_id, show_name, rank (1..N), score, domain, url, title, matched_publication_name (empty if none), and matched_keywords (comma-separated). Include up to the top 5 scored results per show, sorted by score descending and then title ascending for ties.

3) Meeting notes with action items:
   - Generate outputs/meeting_notes/YYYY-MM-DD.md (use the date the job runs) that includes for each show:
     - A brief one-paragraph summary of what was found (e.g., number of high-scoring links, any priority publications matched).
     - A list of up to 3 top links with title, domain, and score.
     - Action items as checkboxes. Include at least:
       - If any priority publication matched: "Draft social post with pull-quote; update the show page with the link".
       - If none matched: "Email press outreach to priority publications for this show".
       - Always include: "Assign owner to verify accuracy of titles/links".

4) Scheduled task execution and runnable scripts:
   - Create scripts/run_once.sh that runs the full pipeline end-to-end now (fetch, rank, and write both outputs/aggregated/top_reviews.csv and outputs/meeting_notes/YYYY-MM-DD.md). It should be POSIX-shell compatible and write a log to logs/run.log.
   - Create scheduler/cron.tab containing a daily schedule at 07:30 local time that calls scripts/run_once.sh and appends logs to logs/cron.log. The job should run from the repo/workspace root so relative paths resolve. Do not attempt to install the crontab; just provide this file with the exact cron line.
   - Ensure the pipeline can be invoked via command line without manual steps beyond running scripts/run_once.sh.

Deliverables to produce in this workspace:
- outputs/raw/search_results.jsonl (line-delimited JSON as specified)
- outputs/aggregated/top_reviews.csv (as specified)
- outputs/meeting_notes/YYYY-MM-DD.md (as specified)
- scripts/run_once.sh (executable script that runs the whole pipeline and logs to logs/run.log)
- scheduler/cron.tab (daily at 07:30 calling scripts/run_once.sh and logging to logs/cron.log)
- logs/run.log created by a test run of scripts/run_once.sh

Notes and constraints:
- Use the provided inputs to drive query construction and ranking. The search step must actually perform live Internet queries; do not stub or hardcode results.
- You may implement the scraping and processing in the language of your choice, but ensure scripts/run_once.sh runs everything end-to-end via command line on a typical personal computer with Internet access.
- Keep dependencies minimal and installable without private credentials. If you add dependencies, document the single command needed to install them at the top of scripts/run_once.sh as comments.
- Do not rely on brittle page structures. It is acceptable to parse search result titles, snippets, and links; if some fields are missing for a result, include what you can and proceed.
- All output paths listed above are required and must be created by your run.

Please proceed and run the pipeline once so that the outputs and logs exist under outputs/ and logs/ for verification. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
